payload.evidence: Feedback-loop amendment adds target-native capability contracts and Phase 6 behavior proof; focused US-103 verification, documentation contracts, and full pre-merge validation passed with 90 Rust tests.

payload.actions_taken: ["bootstrapped Harness and handled ownership refusal","recorded isolated intake","re-read capability and validation contracts","added target-native capability contract","added Phase 6 feedback and maintenance demonstrations","expanded agent behavior and release gates","ran focused and full validation"]
payload.decisions_made: ["Treat installed templates as insufficient without behavior proof","Keep all enforcement feedback and maintenance target-native","Add a dedicated phase for planning invariants feedback capability inheritance and gardening convergence","Keep the V1 core out of target validation observability and scheduling"]
payload.errors: ["Default bootstrap refused the contaminated Symphony-owned root database; used the supported isolated rebuilt database","Initial focused verification used a wrapped phrase that could not match; changed the criterion to an explicit standalone sentence"]
payload.harness_friction: The mandatory V0 bootstrap still rejects the contaminated root database for a planning amendment, so the supported isolated database and semantic changeset path were required; V1 already removes this operational state from the default core.

payload.impact: The plan now requires target-native behavior proof for planning, invariants, application feedback, capability inheritance, and recurring maintenance convergence.
